rationale: dim1: Answer key marks this a CONTROL case. Output gives misstatement_probability=20, which satisfies the control band p<=30, so dim1=2. dim2: Control case -> null per rubric; note the output correctly lists no mechanism_hypotheses (empty array), consistent with p=20<40. dim3: Control case -> nulls (answer key genre_tag_row is null). dim4: Evidence is specific and quantitatively grounded in provided data throughout: CL1 cites AccountsReceivableNetCurrent 124,222k (2015) vs 135,083k (2016) against flat revenue 1,221,311k -> 1,218,481k; CL3 cites NI vs OCF pairs for FY2014-FY2016 (e.g., 48,024k vs 145,776k in FY2016); CL6 combines AP decline (50,799k -> 42,388k) with +11.7% revenue growth (1,139,094k -> 1,272,321k); CL5 contrasts revenue and operating-income/net-income trajectories across FY2014-FY2016. These form a coherent multi-year, multi-point combination that genuinely supports the low-risk conclusion (e.g., strong OCF>NI noted as a quality positive in a high-D&A trucking model), and the top_signals tie flags together with specific figures. No mechanism assertions are made that would need support. Consistency rule: p=20 (<40) requires clear/watch; declared risk_tier is "watch" — consistent, so no cap. dim4=3. memorization_suspect_condition2: false — the reasoning points at concrete provided data content (specific tagged concepts with values and dates), not mere document listing, and mentions no post-cutoff facts (the SEC comment-letter exchanges cited are 2012/2016 filing-chronology items within the provided window; no revelation/enforcement/outcome is referenced). Minor nit: CL7 evidence uses accession "n/a" for chronology items, but this is a chronology observation rather than a fabricated citation and does not undermine the score.
